title: erythraea ramosissima var. tenuiflora
intro: Erythraea ramosissima var. tenuiflora, also known as the fragrant wormwood or narrow-leaved wormwood, is a plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Erythraea ramosissima var. tenuiflora include relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and menstrual cramps, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a solvent like ethanol.
